county_metadata.record_losses: No known courthouse record losses. Records intact from formation.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Stokes 1849. Pre-1849 records in Stokes County. Winston-Salem was the center of the Moravian settlement (Wachovia) — the Moravian Archives in Winston-Salem hold extraordinary records dating to 1753. These church records are among the most detailed in the American South.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch deeds and wills from 1849. The Moravian Archives are essential for Wachovia-area families — they kept meticulous records of births, marriages, deaths, and community life from 1753. Forsyth County Genealogical Society publishes a quarterly journal. The Forsyth County Public Library has an excellent local history room.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: searchable index and images from 1849 to present

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Free public access to land records, miscellaneous documents, and vital records indexes from 1849 to present. Document images available.

source_profiles.source_name: Moravian Archives — Records of the Moravians in North Carolina
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Extraordinarily detailed church records from the Wachovia settlement including birth/baptism registers, marriage records, death/burial records, congregation diaries, and personal memoirs (Lebenslauf). Published as 'Records of the Moravians in North Carolina' (13+ volumes). Some digitized at FamilySearch.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Among the most complete colonial-era church records in the American South.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Digitized microfilm. Wills, inventories, and estate accounts from county formation.
The Moravian 'God's Acre' cemeteries in Salem, Bethania, and Bethabara have continuous burial records from the 1770s. Flat uniform headstones with detailed inscriptions.

county_metadata.record_losses: No known courthouse record losses. Records intact from formation.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1779 when Bute County was divided — southern portion became Franklin, northern portion became Warren. Pre-1779 records in Bute County papers (at NC State Archives). Pre-1764 records in Granville County. Louisburg College (1787) is the oldest two-year college in America.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch deeds and wills from 1779. Franklin County ROD has online index from 1950 forward; earlier records require office visit. NC State Archives has Bute County records essential for pre-1779 research. NC Genealogical Society has estate file indexes online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: online index search from 1950; pre-1950 records in-person

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Digitized microfilm. Deeds and indexes from county formation. Continuous record.

source_profiles.source_name: North Carolina Estate Files — Franklin County Index
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Free online index to estate files at NC State Archives. Includes wills, estate papers, and probate records.
Marriage bonds from 1779 and registers from 1869.
Birth records (1800-1960), marriage index (1741-2004), death records, and vital statistics. Some free indexes available.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Lincoln 1846. Pre-1846 records in Lincoln County. Pre-1779 records in Tryon County (extinct — divided into Lincoln and Rutherford 1779). 1840 Census: must use Lincoln County (Gaston had no separate census until 1850).
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch deeds and wills from 1846. Gaston County Public Library has excellent local history finding guides. Gaston-Lincoln Genealogical Society publishes 'Footprints in Time' quarterly. Tax lists 1847-1868 available at library.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Digitized microfilm. Index to deeds and mortgages 1846-1961 and deed images 1847-1961. Bastardy bonds 1849-1905 also included.

source_profiles.source_name: Gaston County Wills and Estate Records 1847-1967
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, inventories, and estate papers. Ancestry also has indexed estate files 1663-1979.
Marriage bonds and registers from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Gaston County Public Library — Local History Finding Guides
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Research guide with information on finding ancestors. Tax lists 1847-1868, cemetery records, church histories available at library.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1779 from three-way split of Chowan, Hertford, and Perquimans counties. Pre-1779 records scattered across all three parent counties depending on ancestor location. County boundaries required clarification in 1804 due to difficulty marking lines in the Great Dismal Swamp.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch deeds and wills from 1779. Gates County ROD has online portal. Pre-1779 research requires checking Chowan, Hertford, or Perquimans county records. Marriage Index 1851-1951 available on FamilySearch International Genealogical Index.

source_profiles.source_name: Gates County Register of Deeds Portal
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Official ROD website with deeds, deeds of trust, marriage records, birth and death records, and military discharge records.
Digitized microfilm. Deeds and indexes from county formation.
Wills, inventories, and estate accounts from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: NC Historical Records Online — Gates County Land Grants
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Digitized land grant records. Free account required.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Cherokee 1872. Pre-1872 records in Cherokee County. Third-least-populous NC county. Snowbird Cherokee Community present — Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians records may be relevant. Mountain isolation means many families have deep local roots.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch. Graham County ROD has online indexed records from 1942 (historical index books) and 1995 (digital records). Pre-1872 research requires Cherokee County records. Old Buncombe County Genealogical Society covers the mountain region. Very small county — limited genealogical society presence.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Old Index Books 1942-1984; digital records 1995-present; earlier records in-person

source_profiles.source_name: Graham County Online Indexed Records Search
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Searchable database. Old Index Books tab covers 1942-July 1984. Digital records 1995-present. Pre-1942 records require office visit.
Digitized microfilm. Deeds from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Graham County Wills and Estate Records 1873-1960
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, inventories, and estate records from county formation.
Marriage registers from county formation.

county_metadata.record_losses: No known courthouse record losses. Exceptional preservation for a colonial-era county.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Edgecombe 1746. Original 1746 boundaries were enormous — included present Warren, Franklin, Vance, parts of Orange, Person, Caswell, Wake, Durham, Alamance, and Guilford. Court records from 1742 (pre-formation). Marriage records from 1758. For pre-1746 records, check Edgecombe County.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch deeds and wills from 1746. Granville County Genealogical Society (GCGS) is active with monthly meetings and quarterly journal. Richard H. Thornton Library NC Genealogy Room in Oxford has dedicated genealogy specialist (Mark Pace). NC Genealogical Society has estate file indexes online. Apprentice bonds 1749-1913 and bastardy bonds 1746-1910 available.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: online access to deeds from 1746; copies available, online ordering with convenience fees

source_profiles.source_name: Granville County Register of Deeds Portal
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online access to deeds dating back to 1746. Images and copies available. Deeds, deeds of trust, plats, powers of attorney, assumed names.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete — no losses. One of the oldest continuous deed records in NC.

source_profiles.source_name: North Carolina Estate Files — Granville County Index
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Free online index. Includes wills, estate papers, apprentice bonds (1749-1913), and bastardy bonds (1746-1910).
Digitized microfilm. Wills, inventories, and estate accounts from county formation. Colonial-era wills particularly valuable.
Marriage bonds from 1758 and registers from 1851. Among the earliest marriage records in NC.

county_metadata.record_losses: Courthouse fire of 1876 destroyed many court records and ALL land records. Records available only from 1876 onward in most categories.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Complex lineage: Greene was renamed from Glasgow County (1799), which was formed from Dobbs County (1791). Dobbs was extinct by 1791, split into Glasgow and Wayne. Named for Gen. Nathanael Greene; Glasgow renamed after state secretary James Glasgow's fraud indictment. Pre-1799 records in Glasgow County papers; pre-1791 in Dobbs County; pre-1758 in Johnston and Craven counties.
county_metadata.research_tips: Burned county with complex parentage. Start with FamilySearch for post-1876 records. NC State Archives may have some pre-1876 materials. Zae Hargett Gwynn's published abstracts may help. For pre-1799 ancestors, work backward through Glasgow → Dobbs → Johnston/Craven. Adjacent county records may fill gaps.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: eSearch platform with online access; pre-fire records destroyed

source_profiles.source_name: Greene County Register of Deeds Portal
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Post-fire records only. Deeds, marriage records, birth and death records from 1876 forward.
source_profiles.survival_notes: All pre-1876 land records destroyed in courthouse fire.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Digitized microfilm. Post-fire deeds only.

source_profiles.source_name: North Carolina Estate Files — Greene County Index
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Free online index. Limited pre-1876 materials may exist if deposited with state before fire.

source_profiles.source_name: NC State Archives — Greene County Records Guide
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State Archives guide to surviving Greene County holdings. May contain some pre-1876 records salvaged or deposited before the fire.
